speakers.name: Alex Chen
speakers.title: Principal Engineer
speakers.bioShort: Principal engineer specializing in distributed systems and developer experience.
speakers.bioFull: Alex Chen is a principal engineer at Nimbus Systems, where they lead platform teams focused on distributed systems, observability, and internal developer tooling. Alex frequently moderates and speaks on panels about technical decision-making, platform ownership, and building resilient engineering cultures.

speakers.name: Jordan Lee
speakers.title: Lead UX Researcher
speakers.bioShort: UX researcher focused on making research approachable for cross-functional teams.
speakers.bioFull: Jordan Lee leads UX research at Brightline Studio. With a background in psychology and product strategy, Jordan has developed lightweight research frameworks that help teams of any size make evidence-based decisions. Jordan teaches beginner-friendly UX research workshops and mentors aspiring researchers.

speakers.name: Taylor Morgan
speakers.title: Product Manager
speakers.bioShort: Product manager bridging design, data, and engineering teams.
speakers.bioFull: Taylor Morgan works as a product manager at Northwind Apps, focusing on collaboration between design, data, and engineering. Taylor has shipped features used by millions of users and is passionate about roadmapping, stakeholder communication, and outcome-focused product planning.

speakers.name: Samira Khan
speakers.title: Director of AI
speakers.bioShort: Director of AI building responsible machine learning products at scale.
speakers.bioFull: Samira Khan leads the AI organization at Aurora Labs, where her team delivers production ML systems for personalization, search, and recommendations. She regularly speaks about responsible AI practices, evaluation frameworks, and aligning AI investments with business outcomes.

speakers.name: Maria Garcia
speakers.title: Head of Product Design
speakers.bioShort: Design leader focused on systems thinking and inclusive design.
speakers.bioFull: Maria Garcia heads the product design team at Flowline. With over a decade of experience, Maria has built design systems, led large redesigns, and coached cross-functional teams on inclusive design practices that work across platforms and devices.

speakers.name: Daniel Okafor
speakers.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
speakers.bioShort: Frontend engineer working on large-scale design systems and performance.
speakers.bioFull: Daniel Okafor is a senior frontend engineer at Helio Cloud. He specializes in scalable component architectures, web performance optimization, and accessibility. Daniel speaks frequently about bridging the gap between design systems and implementation details.

speakers.name: Lena Sørensen
speakers.title: DevOps Architect
speakers.bioShort: DevOps architect focused on reliability, incident response, and platform teams.
speakers.bioFull: Lena Sørensen designs DevOps practices and platform architectures for CoastalTech. Her work centers on building reliable delivery pipelines, automated testing strategies, and effective incident response processes that empower teams to ship quickly and safely.

speakers.name: Oliver Martin
speakers.title: Conference Host
speakers.bioShort: Professional host and moderator for technology conferences.
speakers.bioFull: Oliver Martin is a seasoned conference host who has facilitated keynotes, panels, and live Q&A sessions at events around the world. Known for his thoughtful questions and steady presence, Oliver helps audiences connect with complex topics and speakers.

speakers.name: Priya Narayan
speakers.title: Data Scientist
speakers.bioShort: Data scientist specializing in experimentation and causal inference.
speakers.bioFull: Priya Narayan is a data scientist at Insightful AI, where she works on experimentation platforms, causal inference, and product analytics. She enjoys helping product and design teams interpret data responsibly and make confident decisions.

speakers.name: Hiro Tanaka
speakers.title: Staff Backend Engineer
speakers.bioShort: Backend engineer focusing on APIs, reliability, and scaling architectures.
speakers.bioFull: Hiro Tanaka is a staff backend engineer at Skyline. He has led multiple efforts to re-architect legacy monoliths into service-oriented platforms, and speaks often about API design, observability, and building for failure.

speakers.name: Chloé Dubois
speakers.title: Service Designer
speakers.bioShort: Service designer focused on end-to-end customer journeys.
speakers.bioFull: Chloé Dubois works as a service designer at Atelier UX, helping organizations design consistent experiences that span channels and departments. She brings together qualitative research, journey mapping, and cross-functional facilitation.

speakers.name: Rafael Silva
speakers.title: Cloud Architect
speakers.bioShort: Cloud architect helping teams migrate and scale on modern infrastructure.
speakers.bioFull: Rafael Silva is a cloud architect at NebulaWorks, where he designs multi-region architectures and migration plans for large enterprises. His sessions cover practical tradeoffs between cost, reliability, and time-to-market.

speakers.name: Emma Roberts
speakers.title: Community Manager
speakers.bioShort: Community manager growing healthy and inclusive developer communities.
speakers.bioFull: Emma Roberts manages community programs at OpenSource Hub. She designs contributor journeys, runs mentorship programs, and facilitates inclusive community spaces online and at events.

speakers.name: Miguel Fernandez
speakers.title: Sr. Engineering Manager
speakers.bioShort: Engineering manager focused on sustainable team practices and delivery.
speakers.bioFull: Miguel Fernandez leads engineering teams at BrightPath. His work centers on creating sustainable delivery practices, coaching engineers into leadership roles, and aligning technical roadmaps with business strategy.

speakers.name: Sophia Ali
speakers.title: UX Writer
speakers.bioShort: UX writer helping teams craft clear, inclusive product copy.
speakers.bioFull: Sophia Ali is a UX writer at Narrative UX. She collaborates closely with designers and PMs to build content guidelines, streamline complex flows, and make products more accessible through language.

speakers.name: Ivana Petrova
speakers.title: Accessibility Specialist
speakers.bioShort: Accessibility specialist advocating for inclusive digital products.
speakers.bioFull: Ivana Petrova works with teams to integrate accessibility into their design and development processes. She provides training, audits, and coaching on WCAG compliance and inclusive design practices.

speakers.name: Liam Nguyen
speakers.title: Site Reliability Engineer
speakers.bioShort: SRE focusing on observability, SLIs, and error budgets.
speakers.bioFull: Liam Nguyen is an SRE at CoreSignal. He works on observability platforms, availability SLOs, and incident management. Liam enjoys translating reliability concepts into practices teams can adopt incrementally.

speakers.name: Nora Hassan
speakers.title: Head of Product
speakers.bioShort: Head of Product leading cross-functional product strategy and discovery.
speakers.bioFull: Nora Hassan leads product at Parallel Products. She has built and scaled product teams, introduced continuous discovery habits, and coached PMs on strategic thinking and stakeholder alignment.

faq_articles.title: What lunch and catering options are available during the conference?
faq_articles.content:
We provide a buffet-style lunch on all three main conference days, along with morning and afternoon coffee breaks.

Our standard lunch menus always include at least one **vegetarian** main dish, a selection of salads, and non-alcoholic beverages. Ingredients and common allergens are labeled at each buffet station.

If you have specific dietary needs (for example, vegan, gluten-free, or food allergies), we recommend indicating them during registration or by contacting our support team at least 10 days before the event so we can work with the venue to accommodate you.

Snacks and coffee/tea are available throughout the day in the networking lounge.

faq_articles.title: Can you accommodate dietary restrictions or allergies?
faq_articles.content:
Yes. We work closely with our catering partners to accommodate common dietary restrictions, including vegetarian, dairy-free, and nut-free options.

When you register, you can indicate your dietary needs in the **Dietary Requirements** section. If your requirements are complex or medically necessary, please also reach out via the support form on our Help & FAQ page so we can confirm what is possible.

While we strive to offer options such as vegan and gluten-free meals, availability may vary by day and is subject to the venue's capabilities. Allergen information is posted at each station, but attendees with severe allergies should exercise caution and may wish to bring backup options.

faq_articles.title: How do I transfer my ticket to another attendee?
faq_articles.content:
If you can no longer attend, you may transfer your ticket to another person until 48 hours before Day 1 of the conference.

To transfer your ticket:
1. Locate your original registration confirmation email.
2. Click the **Manage Registration** link.
3. Select **Transfer Ticket** and enter the new attendee's name, email address, and company.
4. The new attendee will receive a confirmation email to complete their profile and dietary preferences.

If you encounter issues, contact our support team via the **Contact Support** form.

faq_articles.title: What time should I arrive for check-in?
faq_articles.content:
On-site registration and badge pickup open at 7:30 AM on Day 1 and 8:00 AM on Days 2 and 3.

We recommend arriving at least 45 minutes before your first session or workshop to allow time for badge pickup, security checks, and navigating to the correct room.

Remember to bring a government-issued photo ID and your registration confirmation (digital or printed).

faq_articles.title: How do I build and manage My Schedule?
faq_articles.content:
The **My Schedule** feature lets you bookmark sessions, workshops, and networking events.

To add an item to your schedule, open its detail page and click **Add to My Schedule**. If a session supports multiple attendance modes (for example, in person or live stream), you may be asked to choose one.

To remove an item, open its detail page and click **Remove from My Schedule**. If you add overlapping sessions, the app may display a **schedule conflict** warning, but it will not prevent you from saving both. You can resolve conflicts by removing one of the overlapping sessions and choosing an alternative in the same time slot.

Your saved items are visible on the **My Schedule** page and in the schedule view when the **Added to My Schedule** filter is enabled.

faq_articles.title: Will sessions be recorded or live streamed?
faq_articles.content:
Many keynotes and select breakout sessions will be live streamed and recorded.

Sessions that are live streamed show a **Live Stream Available** badge in the schedule. Capacity limits may still apply to interactive features such as Q&A or breakout rooms.

Recordings for eligible sessions are typically posted within 7–10 business days after the conference. Access instructions will be emailed to registered attendees and posted on the Help & FAQ page.

faq_articles.title: Where is the conference venue and how do I get there?
faq_articles.content:
The conference takes place at the Harborfront Convention Center, 123 Seaview Ave.

Public transit, rideshare drop-off points, and parking details are provided on the **About the Conference** page. We recommend using public transportation during peak hours, as on-site parking is limited.

Wayfinding signs and volunteers will direct you from the main entrance to registration and session rooms.

faq_articles.title: What accessibility services are available?
faq_articles.content:
We are committed to making the conference accessible.

Services include step-free access to all main areas, reserved seating for attendees with mobility needs, live captioning for keynotes, and by-request captioning for selected sessions. Service animals are welcome.

If you require specific accommodations (such as ASL interpretation or reserved seating), please let us know via the **Contact Support** form at least 14 days before the event.

sessions.title: Opening Keynote: The Future of Human-Centered AI
sessions.subtitle: Balancing innovation with responsibility
sessions.description: Our opening keynote explores how AI can augment human decision-making while preserving trust and agency. Hear real-world stories from organizations applying AI responsibly at scale.

sessions.title: Designing Onboarding Flows that Stick
sessions.subtitle: Helping new users find value in the first 5 minutes
sessions.description: Learn practical patterns for designing onboarding flows that respect user context, reduce friction, and create lasting engagement. We’ll review examples, metrics, and common pitfalls.

sessions.title: Building Reliable APIs at Scale
sessions.subtitle: Design principles for long-lived services
sessions.description: This session covers patterns for designing and operating APIs that remain reliable as your product and organization grow. We’ll dive into versioning, observability, and failure isolation.

sessions.title: Design Systems in Practice
sessions.subtitle: Making components work for real teams
sessions.description: Explore how to roll out and maintain design systems across multiple products. We’ll discuss governance models, adoption strategies, and collaboration with engineering.

sessions.title: Keynote: Building Product Teams that Learn Fast
sessions.subtitle: Creating a culture of experimentation
sessions.description: This keynote shares stories and frameworks from organizations that have embraced continuous discovery and experimentation, and how their teams make decisions quickly without sacrificing quality.

sessions.title: Panel: Scaling Platform Teams
sessions.subtitle: Where to draw the lines between product and platform
sessions.description: Engineering leaders and staff engineers discuss how they structure platform teams, define ownership boundaries, and prioritize internal customer needs.

sessions.title: Evening Welcome Reception
sessions.subtitle: Meet fellow attendees over drinks and light bites
sessions.description: Kick off the conference with an informal networking reception featuring light snacks and beverages. Open to all registered attendees.

sessions.title: Keynote: Designing for Everyone
sessions.subtitle: Inclusive design at scale
sessions.description: An exploration of inclusive design practices, featuring case studies on making digital products more accessible and welcoming to diverse audiences.

sessions.title: Panel: Microservices vs. Monoliths — Making the Right Tradeoffs
sessions.subtitle: Real stories from teams who have switched (both ways)
sessions.description: Panelists share their experiences breaking up monoliths, consolidating microservices, and knowing when to stop refactoring. Learn how to choose an architecture that matches your context.

sessions.title: From Insights to Impact: UX Research Case Studies
sessions.subtitle: What happens after the study ends
sessions.description: A series of short case studies showing how research insights influenced product decisions, with a focus on communicating findings in a way stakeholders can act on.

sessions.title: Lightning Talks: Developer Tools Showcase
sessions.subtitle: New tools and workflows in 5-minute demos
sessions.description: A fast-paced hour of lightning talks where developers and sponsors share tooling tips, new products, and workflow improvements.

sessions.title: Product & Design Mixer
sessions.subtitle: Connect with PMs, designers, and researchers
sessions.description: An informal evening mixer for product and design professionals. Find collaborators, share stories, and expand your network.

sessions.title: Panel: Observability in Practice
sessions.subtitle: What teams actually monitor (and why)
sessions.description: SREs and engineers walk through the dashboards, alerts, and traces they rely on day-to-day, and how they avoid alert fatigue while keeping systems reliable.

sessions.title: AI Patterns for Legacy Systems
sessions.subtitle: Introducing machine learning without rewriting everything
sessions.description: Learn how to layer AI capabilities onto existing systems using proven integration patterns, from batch scoring to sidecar services and feature stores.

sessions.title: Migrating to Event-Driven Architectures
sessions.subtitle: Practical steps for teams just getting started
sessions.description: This talk outlines a pragmatic path from request/response systems toward event-driven designs, including messaging choices, schema evolution, and common gotchas.

sessions.title: Fireside Chat: Leading Through Change
sessions.subtitle: Supporting teams during reorgs and strategy shifts
sessions.description: Engineering and product leaders discuss how they communicate during times of uncertainty, maintain trust, and keep work moving when priorities change.

sessions.title: Closing Party
sessions.subtitle: Celebrate the week with music and conversation
sessions.description: Wrap up the conference with music, dessert, and informal networking. Open to all full-conference pass holders.

workshops.title: UX Research Fundamentals: Planning Your First Study
workshops.subtitle: A beginner-friendly introduction to UX research
workshops.description: This hands-on workshop walks you through the end-to-end process of planning and running your first UX research study, from defining questions to sharing findings.
workshops.objectives: By the end of this workshop, you will be able to choose an appropriate research method, write a lightweight research plan, recruit participants ethically, and synthesize basic findings.

workshops.title: Practical UX Research on a Budget
workshops.subtitle: Scrappy methods for small teams
workshops.description: Learn how to run effective UX research when time and resources are limited. We’ll cover lean methods, stakeholder alignment, and turning quick insights into product decisions.
workshops.objectives: Participants will practice planning low-cost studies, choosing tools that fit their context, and communicating tradeoffs to stakeholders.

workshops.title: Advanced Survey Design for UX Research
workshops.subtitle: Designing surveys that produce trustworthy data
workshops.description: A deep dive into writing effective survey questions, avoiding bias, and analyzing quantitative UX data.
workshops.objectives: You will learn to identify common survey pitfalls, design better scales, and interpret results responsibly.

workshops.title: Design Systems Bootcamp
workshops.subtitle: Foundations, tokens, and components
workshops.description: A practical workshop on creating and maintaining a design system that works for design and engineering.
workshops.objectives: Define your design system’s goals, create a token strategy, and plan collaboration with engineering.

workshops.title: Kubernetes for Application Developers
workshops.subtitle: From containers to production
workshops.description: Hands-on introduction to deploying and operating applications on Kubernetes for developers new to the ecosystem.
workshops.objectives: By the end, you’ll deploy sample services, configure basic resources, and understand core Kubernetes concepts.

workshops.title: AI Product Strategy Lab
workshops.subtitle: Identifying valuable AI opportunities
workshops.description: A strategy-focused workshop for product leaders who want to evaluate and prioritize AI opportunities in their roadmaps.
workshops.objectives: Participants will map AI use cases to business outcomes and draft a lightweight AI product strategy.

workshops.title: Continuous Product Discovery in Practice
workshops.subtitle: Habits that help teams learn every week
workshops.description: Explore practical routines for product discovery, including regular customer conversations, assumption mapping, and opportunity solution trees.
workshops.objectives: Leave with a weekly discovery cadence and simple artifacts you can bring back to your team.

workshops.title: Frontend Performance Tuning Lab
workshops.subtitle: Measuring and improving real user performance
workshops.description: Hands-on workshop where you’ll profile real applications, interpret performance data, and implement optimizations.
workshops.objectives: Understand core web performance metrics, measure them, and apply tactics to improve them.

workshops.title: Leading Sustainable Engineering Teams
workshops.subtitle: Practices for healthy, high-performing groups
workshops.description: Learn frameworks and rituals that support sustainable pace, trust, and continuous improvement on engineering teams.
workshops.objectives: Create a draft operating model for your team and identify practices to start, stop, and continue.

workshops.title: Facilitating Remote Workshops
workshops.subtitle: Keeping distributed teams engaged
workshops.description: Techniques and tools for running engaging remote workshops and collaborative sessions with distributed teams.
workshops.objectives: Design remote-friendly agendas, choose tools that fit your needs, and handle common facilitation challenges.
